subject: LU-18728 tests: use urandom to really consume ZFS space

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Hi Bruno, thanks for the patch.

You can use the "Test-Parameters:" keyword to change the testing for a patch.

https://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/PUB/Changing+Test+Parameters+with+Gerrit+Commit+Messages

The "trivial" keyword can be used to reduce testing for patches that do not affect code functionality, and then add in specific ZFS test sessions for the modified subtests:

 Test-Parameters: trivial
 Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=32,FSTYPE=zfs
 Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity env=ONLY=413,FSTYPE=zfs

Note that WC autotest and Gerrit Janitor will already run extra sessions for subtests that are modified, so in some cases it is enough to only specify "trivial" for patches that are modifying a particular subtest, as can be seen by the Maloo comment posted when testing is started:

  The following sessions will be run for build 110898 patch 6
  :
  * review-dne-subtest-change on el8.10-x86_64/ldiskfs servers and clients
  * review-dne-zfs-subtest-change on el8.10-x86_64/zfs servers and clients

Patch Set 1:

Note however, that the ZFS default compression was disabled during testing in a recent patch, so this change won't have any visible effect, but is still useful in case this is re-enabled in the future.

Patch Set 1:

BUILD

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Please don't turn on compression by default. Oleg's setup will start having various failures due to timeouts.

Patch Set 4:

You can pass in <facet_type>_FS_MKFS_OPTS="compression=on" using env="" if you want to test with compression on. Personally I would do that in a follow on separate patch to ensure with compression on and off these changes work.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1
> 
> Please don't turn on compression by default. Oleg's setup will start having various failures due to timeouts.

I think he is just trying to test whether the patch works.

Patch Set 5:

> Please don't turn on compression by default. Oleg's setup will
 > start having various failures due to timeouts.

I would rather have mkfs.lustre leave compression enabled by default for production usage, and have test-framework.sh always set compression=off.  That leaves ZFS with the default behavior, but avoids the testing issues that are currently being hit.

That said, this can be changed in a separate patch once we know that testing is passing consitently.

parents.subject: LU-18694 sec: nodemap local root user capabilities

commit.subject: LU-18728 tests: use urandom to really consume ZFS space
commit.message:
LU-18728 tests: use urandom to really consume ZFS space

It appears newer ZFS is using data compression by default, so reading
from /dev/zero results in files not consuming the expected amount of
space.  Instead, read from /dev/urandom for ZFS to write files in
sanity and conf-sanity to ensure they fill the OSTs, or the image
to be used for target creation, as expected.

Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-zfs env=ZFS_MKFS_OPTS="compression=on"
Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bfaccini@nvidia.com>
Change-Id: I7b4e95032608d8db82c75e4b6dd1ec5beb6f8d99
